zh.lifeSummary: Thomas Fuller是John Fuller IV和Margaret的兒子，於1374年出生在英國素福爾可波利 聖 艾德蒙茲。他於大約1397年與Elizabeth Mobberly在英國素福爾可亞可利結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1439年在他的家鄉享年65歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭素福爾可波利 聖 艾德蒙茲聖 艾德蒙達斯 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Fuller
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a dresser of cloth, from Middle English fuller ‘fuller of cloth’ (partly from Old English fullere, partly from Old French fouleor, foleur, Latin fullo). Raw cloth had to be fulled, i.e. scoured, cleansed, and thickened by beating or trampling it in water, a process also known as walking or tucking, hence the surnames Walker and Tucker alongside Fuller. These three terms and surnames are characteristic of different parts of England. In general, in Middle English, Fuller is southern and eastern, while Walker belongs to the west and north and Tucker is southwestern. Compare Fullen .
English: variant of Fullard with loss of -d.
German (Füller): occupational name for a person whose work involved filling, such as a dauber, or a nickname for a gourmand or glutton. Compare Filler .

variants.name: Walker
meanings: English (mainly North and Midlands) and Scottish: occupational name for a fuller, from Middle English walker, Old English wealcere (an agent derivative of wealcan ‘to walk, tread’), ‘one who trampled cloth in a bath of lye or kneaded it, in order to strengthen it’. This was the regular term for the occupation during the Middle Ages in western and northern England. Compare Fuller and Tucker . As a Scottish surname it has also been used as a translation of Gaelic Mac an Fhucadair ‘son of the fuller’. This surname is also very common among African Americans.

History: The name was brought to North America from northern England and Scotland independently by many different bearers in the 17th and 18th centuries. Samuel Walker came to Lynn, MA, c. 1630; Philip Walker was in Rehoboth, MA, in or before 1643. The surname was also established in VA before 1650; a Thomas Walker, born in 1715 in King and Queen County, VA, was a physician, soldier, and explorer.

variants.name: Filler
meanings: South German: occupational name for a flayer or skinner, from Middle High German viller ‘knacker’, from villen ‘to skin’.

variants.name: Fullen
meanings: English: occupational name for a fuller (see Fuller ), from Old French fulun ‘fuller’.
English (Lancashire): branch of this family in Lancashire probably represents a variant of Fulham (see Fullam ), reintroduced from Ireland.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: Thomas Fuller was born in 1374, in Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, England as the son of John Fuller IV and Margaret. He married Elizabeth Mobberly about 1397, in Yaxley, Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1439, in his hometown, at the age of 65, and was buried in St Edmund's Church, Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom.

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Fuller(은)는 1374년 영국 수폴크 버이 스트 에드먼드즈에서 John Fuller IV(와)과 Margaret의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 대략 1397년 영국 수폴크 야스리에서 Elizabeth Mobberly(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1439년 그의 고향에서 65세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 버이 스트 에드먼드즈 스트 에드먼드'스 c후르치에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Mobberly aproximadamente 1397, em Yaxley, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1439, em sua cidade natal, com 65 anos, e foi sepultado em St Edmund's Church, Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Thomas Fuller родился в 1374 г. в местности бури ст эдмундс, суффолк, Англия. Его родителей звали John Fuller IV и Margaret. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Mobberly около 1397 г. в местности яксли, суффолк, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1439 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 65 лет и был похоронен в местности ст эдмунд'с чурч, бури ст эдмундс, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания.
